novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 1 | Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The checklist misses Denteh and Kedagni, Misclassification in Difference-in-Differences Models, revised 2026, whose abstract explicitly covers ambiguous policy timing, DID misclassification, wrong signs, attenuation, and ATT bounds; the proposal must position Gamma_lambda(P) against that paper.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Add DentehKedagni2026 to Related work and the Paragraph 4 gap clauses, with a location anchor to the ambiguous-timing/wrong-sign/bounds claim and a precise distinction from exact-event-time refinement support.

Add a short algorithmic corollary for computing Gamma_lambda(P): input table, transportation constraints, denominator lower bounds, and whether the finite extreme-point search is claimed only for single-denominator cases or for all contrasts.

For Conjecture 1, split the endpoint certificate into the proven compactness-attainment part and the stronger extreme-point-attainment part, so Stage 0 can fail or prove the stronger claim without endangering the sharp-set definition.

recommended_next_step: Revise the literature positioning around DentehKedagni2026 and sharpen the computational certificate claim; the repo novelty kernel survives.
